claim: Polylogue can run and preserve a construct-valid pilot comparing a bounded handoff packet against a raw session reference for a continuation reconstruction task.
non_claim: This n=1 pilot does not prove general agent uplift, does not isolate model stochasticity, and does not replace the planned n=12-20 paired protocol.
proof: protocol.json fixes the arms, task, context rules, metrics, and construct-validity limits before arm outputs are scored
arm prompts are preserved with agent ids in protocol.json
outputs and scoring are stored under current/arms and current/metrics
caveat: the run is susceptible to repository-environment contamination because both agents have normal local tools
the pilot uses self-reported secondary metrics unless later archive capture is joined in
